title: Verify that used OCI runtime supports checkpoint
body:
To be able to use OCI runtimes which do not implement checkpoint/restore
this adds a check to the checkpoint code path and the checkpoint/restore
tests to see if it knows about the checkpoint subcommand. If the used
OCI runtime does not implement checkpoint/restore the tests are skipped
and the actual 'podman container checkpoint' returns an error.

Signed-off-by: Adrian Reber <areber@redhat.com>

comments.body: @giuseppe Would something like this work for detecting a runtime that does not implement checkpoint/restore? Not sure if this is the right place for the check.

/approve

Idea makes a lot of sense, implementation mostly looks solid (I do wonder a bit about different CLI libraries dealing with `-h` as opposed to `--help` (I suppose this doesn't matter if the only real runtime supporting it for now is `runc` - we can add handling for others later, if necessary)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *adrianreber*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM, but as @mheon said, we probably want it for restore too
